commit.message:
v2: Fix scraper extracting platform-level links instead of individual job URLs

Problem: v1's generic extractor grabbed navigation links ("产品与技术"),
category labels ("技术运营类"), and JD text fragments as job entries.
ByteDance returned 0 real jobs, Tencent had no URLs, MiniMax had 30+
duplicate fragments.

Fix:
- Add platform-specific extractors for Feishu, ByteDance, Tencent
- URL pattern filtering: only keep /position/{ID}, /job_detail/{ID} etc
- Click-through for SPA cards that use click handlers instead of <a> tags
- Auto-pagination (default 3 pages)
- Proper deduplication by URL or title
- Structured field extraction (title, location, department, jobType)

New difficulty section added to README (Difficulty #8).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: remove transition banner and update scope descriptions (#551)

* refactor: rename CAPZ_USER to CAPI_USER for provider neutrality (fixes #549)

Rename the CAPZ_USER environment variable and related Go identifiers to
CAPI_USER, since the user prefix is not Azure-specific and applies
equally to ROSA/CAPA deployments.

Changes:
- Rename env var CAPZ_USER → CAPI_USER (with backward-compatible fallback)
- Rename Go constant DefaultCAPZUser → DefaultCAPIUser
- Rename Go struct field CAPZUser → CAPIUser
- Add getCAPIUser() helper: prefers CAPI_USER, falls back to CAPZ_USER
- Update Makefile: CAPI_USER with CAPZ_USER fallback via ifdef
- Update cleanup script: CAPI_USER with CAPZ_USER fallback
- Update all documentation (30 files)

Backward compatibility:
- Setting CAPZ_USER still works in Go code, Makefile, and scripts
- No breaking changes for existing CI/CD pipelines or user configs

Fixes #549

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove CAPZ_USER backward compatibility

Remove all CAPZ_USER fallback logic — the old name is no longer
supported. CAPI_USER is now the only accepted env var name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove transition banner and update scope descriptions

Remove the outdated "formerly ARO-CAPZ Test Suite" transition banner
and update ARO-only scope descriptions to reflect the current
multi-provider (ARO + ROSA) support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
refactor: production code audit — fix critical bugs and data consistency

Critical fixes:
- Enable automod checkMessage() (was commented out, never running)
- Cache banned words in-memory (was querying DB on every message)
- Move dailyMessageCounts to Redis (was in-memory, reset on restart)
- Fix Redis connection error propagation (was silently swallowed)
- Fix poll guild lookup to use config.guildId (was cache.first())

Data consistency fixes:
- Fix N+1 query in digest leaderboard (batch user fetch)
- Fix interaction dedup memory leak (time-based expiry vs unbounded set)
- Remove deprecated promoteMember/demoteMember methods
- Remove @types/ioredis v4 (ioredis v5 bundles own types)
- Fix ActionRowBuilder any types in button disable logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(preprod): Post build distribution comments to GitHub PRs (#109476) ## Summary - Adds a Celery task that posts (or updates) a comment on GitHub PRs when installable builds finish processing - Includes a markdown template for rendering build info (app name, version, config) with iOS/Android support - Links in the comment point to the install page for each artifact - Triggered automatically from `_assemble_preprod_artifact_installable_app` after installable app assembly completes - Supports both GitHub and GitHub Enterprise integrations - Gated behind the `organizations:preprod-build-distribution-pr-comments` feature flag - Uses `select_for_update` on the commit comparison row to serialize concurrent tasks, preventing duplicate comments when multiple builds finish at the same time (e.g., monorepo with iOS + Android) - Stores PR comment state on `CommitComparison.extras` rather than individual artifacts - Preserves the `comment_id` on API failure so retries update the existing comment instead of creating duplicates - Adds `format_version_string()` to `PreprodArtifactMobileAppInfo` for rendering version info in the comment In follow up PR we will add the settings toggle for this. Here's what this looks like visually. It links to the `install` page for the artifact. Here's a link to a test comment the script created with a 60 second delay between the builds. You can see that it updated the comment twice: https://github.com/runningcode/Test-github-sentry/pull/1#issuecomment-3996213802 EME-795 --------- Co-authored-by: Claude

commit.message:
v7: JS number treatment - Chrome bypasses registry tel: handler

Chrome sends tel: URIs directly to MicroSIP instead of going through
the Windows registry protocol handler (branorte-dial.bat). This caused
MicroSIP to receive raw numbers like 554898313374 instead of the
treated 998313374.

Fix: Port dial-treat.ps1 logic to JavaScript treatNumber() function.
The tel: href now contains the already-treated number, so even when
Chrome bypasses the bat file, MicroSIP gets the correct number.

Treatment: strip 55, identify DDD 48, add 9th digit for mobile,
route local (subscriber only) vs interurbano (0+DDD+sub).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[C] balance(SPEC-RC-002 Round2): sprintHPDrainMultiplier 3→2, reckoner_baseRate 1.1→0.7

MCP 시뮬레이션 Round 2 검증 완료 (기대 순위 전 거리 달성):
  2바퀴: 선행>도주>선입>추입(탈진) ✅
  3바퀴: 선행>선입>추입(탈진)>도주(탈진) ✅
  5바퀴: 추입>선입>선행>도주(탈진) ✅

변경 사유:
  sprintHPDrainMultiplier 3→2: 5바퀴 추입 93%소모 → 부스트 곡선 과도진입 수정
  reckoner_baseRate 1.1→0.7: 추입이 스프린트 진입 시 HP 더 여유있게 보존
  lapFactor = hpLapRef(3) / laps → 실질배율: 2바퀴×3.0 / 3바퀴×2.0 / 5바퀴×1.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Phase 8: Calm timer session — timestamp-based engine, state machine, soft completion

Timestamp-driven session engine with explicit state machine
(idle/running/paused/completed_active/ended). Wall-clock timer keeps
running through background/lock. Minutes-only display with step-based
SVG progress ring. Soft completion: 80% early-complete threshold
(12/24/48 min for 15/30/60 min rituals). Overtime display (+M:SS),
one-time completion haptic, back-button confirmation with threshold logic.

commit.message:
fix: pass config to ScanAllLayers so remote layers resolve correctly

ScanAllLayers(dir) passed nil config, so CollectRemoteRefs found no
remote refs and remote layers were never downloaded. This caused
`ov list targets` to fail with "unknown layer" for fedora-remote,
making the CI build loop iterate zero times (silent no-op).

Also hardens CI build script with set -euo pipefail so ov list targets
errors are no longer silently swallowed.

test: remove log_type=application filter — send all logs to Splunk

Bypass splunk_filter stage so all container logs (system + application)
forward directly to the OTEL/Splunk HEC pipeline. Makes it easy to
verify end-to-end delivery in Splunk regardless of log_type.
